raw_form_input.scenario_summary: Married applicant is pregnant with twins and lives with her spouse. Their combined income is too high for SNAP, LIHEAP, and standard adult Medicaid, but the pregnant applicant may still qualify for Medicaid because unborn children increase household size for that category.
raw_form_input.language_or_stress_notes: Important caveat: pregnancy Medicaid uses unborn children in household size; spouse may not qualify.

household_profile.language_or_stress_notes: Important caveat: pregnancy Medicaid uses unborn children in household size; spouse may not qualify.
household_profile.scenario_summary: Married applicant is pregnant with twins and lives with her spouse. Their combined income is too high for SNAP, LIHEAP, and standard adult Medicaid, but the pregnant applicant may still qualify for Medicaid because unborn children increase household size for that category.

failed_conditions: Household income 4300 exceeds the prototype SNAP threshold 2800.

matched_conditions: Pregnancy increases the effective household size for the prototype Medicaid screen.
caveats: Pregnancy-specific Medicaid may apply even if other adults do not qualify.

matched_conditions: Income 4300 is within the prototype WIC limit 5900.

failed_conditions: No strong local referral trigger was detected in the prototype rules.

uncertainty_flags: Pregnancy-related coverage may apply differently across household members.
priority_rationale: Local Referral ranked with prototype score 45.

WIC: Proof of pregnancy or child age

next_steps: Gather the listed documents for the highest-priority programs first.
Treat this as prescreening only, not an official eligibility determination.
checklist_explanation.user_explanation: Based on the information provided, this household may be a fit for Medicaid/CHIP, WIC. Medicaid/CHIP: Pregnancy increases the effective household size for the prototype Medicaid screen. WIC: Income 4300 is within the prototype WIC limit 5900. Caveats: Pregnancy-related coverage may apply differently across household members. Pregnancy-specific Medicaid may apply even if other adults do not qualify. This is a prescreening and next-step guide, not an official determination.
visible_caveats: Pregnancy-related coverage may apply differently across household members.
Pregnancy-specific Medicaid may apply even if other adults do not qualify.
